sevenDayForecast.headline: Koji has crossed the coast between Ayr and Bowen and has weakened below tropical cyclone intensity.
points: A Severe Weather Warning and Flood Watches and Warnings are current and should be referred to for more information. A final Forecast Track Map has been issued for Koji (12U).
Koji is forecast to weaken further and move west and then northwest, through the Gulf Country and towards the Northern Territory, then potentially towards northern WA next weekend.
Next weekend if Koji does move off the northern WA coast, it has a chance of redeveloping into a tropical cyclone.

trackForecast.headline: Tropical Cyclone Koji (12U), category 1, lies east northeast of Townsville and will cross the coast and weaken this morning.
trackForecast.discussion:
Tropical Cyclone Koji (12U) lies the Coral Sea northeast of Townsville as a broad monsoonal system. Koji is moving south and will cross the coast between Ayr and Bowen in the next 3-5 hours this morning.

The Abbott Point area is currently under a Tropical Cyclone Warning. A brief period of gales with damaging wind gusts to 100 km/h are possible this morning. Heavy to intense rainfall is also possible in the area today.

After crossing the coast, the winds of the system will quickly weaken and the risk of gales should rapidly ease. Heavy rain may continue through Sunday and Monday.
trackForecast.discussionUpperBound: The upper bound is based on the system slowly developing until landfall, rather than maintaining intensity as is currently forecast, and being slower to reach the coast thus having more time to develop.
